comments.body: show full stack trace. And check the accept and content type. it should match with what is defined by the API_URI
when i do the call manually in post man directly to remote URI it works with content type json.. but in my java code doesn't :/
Hard to tell if we don't know the specifics of the requested resource. Is the resource also developed by you? Could you provide that code?

no i didn't develop it, but i can execute it normally in post man with this params: URI: same as the one i use in java Content-Type: application/json  and those params as json: { "param1": true, "param2": false }  that's it
We should have a look to the endpoint logs. This is just a generic exception.

What is the expected type for param1 and param2? In postman you send boolean values but in your java code you pass them as string values. May be the server is rejecting your request because of parsing errors
i just hided names, i sent same example as postman
Turn on TRACE level logging on org.apache.http.wire logger to see what RestTemplate is actually sending to the URL. The difference should be obvious to you then.
didn't aswell, by the way the rest api can be executed in post man only by mentioning Content type.

Yes,postman default the accept header is application/json,I beleive this issue is due to the data type conversion problem for boolean as somebosy mentioned above.Try sending only with one parameter excluding the param2,with this we can crosscheck!
same error, response that comes from server is json formatted but can be returned as string also in java
Are you using any authorization for this REST api while hitting from Postman?
This problem Http400 will come only with wrong data or wrong headers!

answers.body:
Can you try removing Accept header,as it is default json

headers.add("Accept", MediaType.APPLICATION_JSON.toString());

OR

try adding accept header as below

headers.setAccept(Collections.singletonList(MediaType.APPLICATION_JSON));

And check what is the response-type object you have mentioned as String.class,please crosscheck

Can you try changing as below

    headers.setAccept(Arrays.asList(MediaType.APPLICATION_JSON));
MultiValueMap<String, Object> requestBody = new LinkedMultiValueMap<>();
        requestBody.add("param1", "value1");
        requestBody.add("param2", true);Try this?

title: Getting bad request when using rest template
body:
I keep getting bad request error when trying to use RestTemplate. I've tried both getForObject() and exchange() methods and neither did work, so am I doing something wrong ?

HttpHeaders headers = new HttpHeaders();
headers.setContentType(MediaType.APPLICATION_JSON);
headers.add("Accept", MediaType.APPLICATION_JSON.toString());

MultiValueMap<String, String> requestBody = new LinkedMultiValueMap<>();
requestBody.add("param1", "value1");
requestBody.add("param2", "true");

RestTemplate restTemplate = new RestTemplate();
return restTemplate.exchange(API_URI, HttpMethod.POST,
                    new HttpEntity<>(requestBody, headers), String.class);

stack trace:(hidded api names)
